results.content: 2026 Fact Book The Jockey Club is pleased to introduce the 2026 edition of our annual guide to the Thoroughbred industry in North America. We hope you find the statistics and information contained herein helpful in your research. Although most statistics may be considered final, some are not. For example, data pertaining to 2025 racing, supplied by racetracks of North America and the North American Thoroughbred industry's Official database for racing information, Equibase Company, can be considered final but is subject to revision if additional data becomes available. Because of the cyclical nature of reporting procedures, however, breeding statistics such as the distribution of foals, stallions and mares are subject to change. These statistics are updated daily. (Unless otherwise noted, Canadian dollars have not been converted to U.S. dollars.) If you need additional information about the Thoroughbred breeding or racing industry, please contact our [Communications Department.](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?section=Contact&area=2) THE AMERICAN RACING MANUAL [* 2025 The American Racing Manual](https://jockeyclub.com/factbook/Chronicle/ARM2025.pdf)[* 2024 The American Racing Manual](https://jockeyclub.com/factbook/Chronicle/ARM2024.pdf)[* 2023 The American Racing Manual](https://jockeyclub.com/factbook/Chronicle/2023_arm.pdf)[* The Jockey Club 2022 Racing Chronicle](https://jockeyclub.com/factbook/Chronicle/2022_Chronicle.pdf)[* 2021 The American Racing Manual](https://jockeyclub.com/factbook/Chronicle/2021_arm.pdf) BREEDING [* Annual NA Registered Foal Crop](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?section=FB&area=2)[* NA Distribution of Active Stallions and Mares Bred](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?section=FB&area=33)[* Total NA Thoroughbreds Over 25-Year Periods](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?section=FB&area=25)[* U.S. Distribution of Active Stallions and Mares Bred](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?section=FB&area=3)[* Distribution of Registered U.S. Foal Crop by State](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?section=FB&area=4)[* Percent Mares Bred by Stallion Covering Year](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?section=FB&area=psftMB)[* Percent of Mares Bred Categorized by Stallion Book Size](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?section=Resources&area=24)[* Number of Unique Sires by Position in the Pedigree Top Line](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?section=Resources&area=21)[* Thoroughbred Import/Export Trends](https://jockeyclub.com/default.asp?...

results.title: PDF Econ Analysis (Draft) - NYTHA
results.snippet: The State of Thoroughbred Horse Racing in the United States: An Economic Analysis and the Path to Industry Stability The sport of thoroughbred horse racing was once the most popular sport in the United States. Although large events like the Kentucky Derby and the Belmont Stakes still capture popular interest, the sport has on most days been reduced to a niche market, albeit with a highly ...
results.content: > 1 ## The State of Thoroughbred Horse Racing in the United States: An Economic ## Analysis and the Path to Industry Stability The sport of thoroughbred horse racing was once the most popular sport in the United States. Although large events like the Kentucky Derby and the Belmont Stakes still capture popular interest, the sport has on most days been reduced to a niche market, albeit with a highly interested core audience. In this research paper, we study recent macroeconomic trends as well as microeco nomic decisions that have shaped the sport’s economic structure. The paper’s primary objective is to take a snapshot of the current state of the sport based on 2022 data, and to provide analysis by comparing this data with the same data from the past 20 ye ars, beginning in 2003. By examining these data points and applying simple economic principles (e.g., supply and demand, perfect competition vs . oligopoly/monopolistic markets), we aim to identify and understand the sport’s prevailing secular trends and gain insights into its current economic dynamic. At the heart of our examination is rooting all of our findings and narratives in as much data as possible, leveraging access to an extensive database of information that has remained largely unexplored until now. Surprisingly, despite the sport's rich hist ory and widespread popularity, this kind of comprehensive economic analysis has rarely been attempted. Limited access to this data and the fractured nature of stakeholder ownership and involvement within the sport, namely racetrack operators and thoroughbr ed owners, are two of the main reasons why the state of the sport has so rarely been assessed and altered. We’d like to hope that the information provided within may lead the sport to support more rational decision -making. With that in mind, a quick preview of our findings would suggest: 1. The Great Recession accelerated what had previously been a slow but steady decline in participation. Trends in every category deepened significantly 1 2. We found surprising strength in two areas - bloodstock prices and per capita purse distribution . 3. Signs of strength are primarily based on supply /demand dynamics coupled with increased alternative (casino) revenue funding purse accounts. > 4. Since the Great Recession, owner value proposition has improved due to the combination of less competition and increased purse sizes. > 1 Interestingly, the shock of Covid -19 in 2020 has seemingly had little impact as t...
